framework: Kansei Engineering – Semantic Differential Word Pairs
source: Derived from Kansei Engineering methodology (Nagamachi, M., 1995. Kansei Engineering: A new ergonomic consumer-oriented technology for product development. International Journal of Industrial Ergonomics, 15(1), 3-11). Word pairs curated from lifestyle/apparel/consumer goods studies in the Kansei literature. Not a single published standard list – Kansei vocabulary is always domain-specific by design.
note: Kansei uses BIPOLAR pairs. The model should place the brand on a spectrum between two poles, not pick a single value. Each pair represents one perceptual axis. This list is pre-filtered for fashion/lifestyle/bag products.
usage: For each pair, indicate which pole the brand communication leans toward, or 'neutral' if neither applies. Select max 4 pairs that are most strongly signaled by the text. Return as: {'modern_traditional': 'modern', 'minimal_ornate': 'minimal', ...}
scale_note: Original Kansei uses a 7-point Likert scale. For LLM use: return the dominant pole label or 'neutral'.
for_image_generation: Translate dominant poles directly into visual language: 'minimal+urban+dynamic' → clean composition, city backdrop, motion blur or diagonal framing; 'natural+timeless+refined' → outdoor soft light, neutral palette, still composition
